description: A large kinship group, whether of blood or choice, sharing language, customs, and territory. It represents a wider circle of belonging and mutual responsibility beyond the clan.
rationale: The expansive 'wa' opens arms wide, gathering many into shared belonging - not exclusive but embracing. This wideness connects through 'lo', weaving individual threads into collective tapestry strong enough to weather storms. Together they speak tribe's truth: wide enough to hold diversity, connected enough to act as one. The flowing sounds mirror how tribes form - through openness becoming connection, many becoming whole.
